title: DistilBERT, a distilled version of BERT: smaller, faster, cheaper and lighter
objective: To propose a method for pre-training a smaller, general-purpose language representation model (DistilBERT) to overcome the challenges of operating large pre-trained NLP models under constrained computational budgets, while maintaining good performance on a wide range of tasks.

methodology.approach: The work leverages knowledge distillation during the pre-training phase to reduce the size of a BERT model, aiming to produce a general-purpose model suitable for fine-tuning.

key_findings.finding: The proposed DistilBERT model significantly reduces the size of the original BERT model.
key_findings.evidence: reduce the size of a BERT model by 40%

key_findings.finding: DistilBERT retains a high percentage of the original model's linguistic capabilities despite its reduced size.

conclusion: DistilBERT offers a more efficient alternative to larger pre-trained models, successfully reducing model size by 40% while preserving nearly all of its language representation capabilities, thereby making advanced NLP models more viable for environments with computational constraints.
